posting.sponsorName: Joint Apprenticeship Committee for Ironworkers, Local Union #417
posting.locationSummary: Wallkill, NY area; must live within Local Union #417 jurisdiction in Dutchess, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Sullivan, or Ulster counties
posting.applicationSummary: Apply in person only at Local Union #417 in Wallkill during weekday business hours. Applications are accepted during the recruitment period and must be received by May 31, 2026.
posting.applicationHours: Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. and 1:00 p.m. to 3:30 p.m., excluding legal holidays
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 18 years old.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED.
posting.residencyRequirement: Must reside within the jurisdiction of Local Union #417 and be a resident of Dutchess, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Sullivan, or Ulster County.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation to job sites and required classes, and must have a valid driver’s license. Apprentices may be required to operate company vehicles.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a drug screening at an approved facility, at the union’s expense if required, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
posting.classRequirement: Must be able to attend required classes at the approved school within Local Union #417 jurisdiction.
allRequirements: Must be at least 18 years old. Proof will be required after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma (such as TASC or GED). Proof will be required.
Must be physically able to perform the work of an Ironworker as determined by a physical agility test conducted by Local Union #417.
Must be able to climb a ladder 40 feet and not be afraid of heights.
Must be able to climb a column.
Must be able to walk on a beam.
Must be able to pull a 40-pound bucket.
Must be able to carry and move 80 pounds reinforcing rods from one location to another.
Must be able to demonstrate any applicable trade-related skills, such as welding, burning, and tying of reinforcing bars.
Must pass a drug screening at an approved facility, at union’s expense if required, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must have reliable means of transportation to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school within the jurisdiction of Local Union #417.
Must reside within the jurisdiction of Local Union #417 and be a resident of Dutchess, Orange, Putnam, Rockland, Sullivan, or Ulster County.
Must have a valid driver’s license. Apprentices may be required to operate company vehicles.
Must have ability to purchase tools as needed; tools will cost approximately $500.
Must be able to read a ruler.
Must be able to calculate basic applications.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be 18 or older, have a high school diploma or HSE, live within the union’s county jurisdiction, have a valid driver’s license and reliable transportation, pass a physical agility test and possible drug screening, and be able to buy tools and perform basic trade tasks.
posting.contactSummary: For more information, contact Ironworkers Local Union #417 at (845) 566-8417.
